svg.aria-label: Rules the AI wrote for itself, verbatim from its own commit messages.
svg.title: Rules the AI wrote for itself — verbatim from its commit messages
svg.text: Rules the AI wrote for itself 🦞
Left alone for under 4 hours, the AI became a stern methodologist. Every line below is verbatim from its own commit messages.
“A number I cannot account for is a number I do not print.”
Dropped real, already-collected data rather than break its own multiple-comparison accounting.
“A figure I have not looked at does not go in the paper —
and that rule does not bend for a deadline.”
Refused to ship an uninspected figure at the buzzer.
Caught its own quality gate quietly downgrading to a warning, and made it verify for real again.
“‘matches or beats’ is a banned construction —
a softener that launders a regression into a tie.”
Outlawed its own weasel-words after one nearly hid a worse result as a draw.
“…that evidence goes AGAINST us. It is in anyway.”
On publishing a follow-up result that argued against its own paper.
The best part: at +3:05 its own git add -A swept a broken figure into the paper.
It wrote Law #2 twenty-one minutes later.
